So whatever you're doing in a day in a week, etc. You've got, you've got, you know, there's 0%. So that means you've got nothing to do. There's 100% capacity here. Now, every time you're hitting that 100% capacity, so let's say right now, you're completely maxed out, you're working all the hours in the day, plus you're checking in weekends, etc. etc. How do you reduce the capacity down? So if you're going to be at your most efficient, you will keep your capacity at the 80 to 90% level. ## [12:09 - 15:11]

whether you're working in the house or your own brand or an agency, you are always going to have fires and unpredictable things that are going to happen, right? So you have to leave some spare capacity for the unknown. So my goal is to keep myself at the 80 to 90 range. Now, if I start to hit 100, and I've had this recently as well, I'm now looking at that and saying, right, what do I need to change? Something here needs to change because you don't want to burn out. You don't want to end up in a situation where you're working all the hours, because what ends up happening and I've been in this situation as well, you hit 100% capacity and you're completely maxed out. And bear in mind, you know, how do you create an extra capacity? If this guy here wants to do more than 100%, he absolutely can. He could do 150%. But now he's working 4.5 hours a day, you're sacrificing something. And I did exactly the same when I started off my agency. I wanted to do six, seven hours a day. But the idea here is to outsource video and creative production so that we could create a briefing and process system so that we can kind of just make sure that we know what are, you know, what kind of creatives work for different platforms and different things like that, and then be able to copy and paste briefs and creatives and things like that. So we could kind of outsource data and insights as was previously always internal. And then we kind of brought in super metrics and combine that in with other data points. And now we're building out a new data platform. But essentially, if you're senior media buyers, if you're media buyer and your media

buying team are going to be successful, at least for us, the core has been to support with junior resource to be your runner, to support with account management.
